title: C Variables
content:
### Variables in C

A variable is a named memory location that stores a value. Every variable must be declared with a data type before use.

### Variable Declaration

```
type variableName;
type variableName = value;
```

### Variable Naming Rules

| Rule | Example |
|------|---------|
| Must start with letter or underscore | `myVar`, `_temp` |
| Can contain letters, digits, underscores | `myVar1`, `count_2` |
| Case-sensitive | `age` and `Age` are different |
| Cannot use keywords | `int`, `return` not allowed |
| No spaces allowed | `my var` is invalid |

---

### Variable Initialization

Variables can be initialized at declaration or assigned later. Uninitialized local variables contain garbage values.

---

> **Key Point:** Always initialize variables before using them to avoid undefined behavior from garbage values.
